summary.text:
Error class for failures originating from the chifra daemon, or
from the network transport between this client and the daemon.

-

always holds the chifra endpoint that failed
  (e.g.

is set when the daemon responded with a non-2xx HTTP
  status; absent when the failure was at the transport layer
  (DNS, connection refused, fetch threw).
-

is set when wrapping a transport-layer error so callers
  can drill into the original failure if needed.
Base URL of the chifra daemon, e.g.

.
A trailing slash is allowed and stripped.

. Useful
for tests, custom transports, or environments that need to wrap
the fetch call (auth headers, retry, instrumentation).
True at type level when a chifra endpoint's

parameter is
required (the OpenAPI spec did not mark it optional). Used to
narrow
to a required-arg signature for endpoints like

) while keeping a
no-arg-allowed signature for endpoints like

Extracts the query-parameter type for a given chifra endpoint
from the generated OpenAPI types. The

)
spec definitions, returning the inner object type either way.

Internal request function used by every verb. Verbs are pure
factories that take this and return their typed callable — tests
inject a mock to exercise verb logic without hitting fetch.
Extracts the JSON response body type for a given chifra endpoint.
The callable signature of a verb. Distinguishes endpoints whose
is required (call MUST pass an arg, e.g.
is optional (call MAY omit the arg, e.g.

). Required fields inside the query object remain
required regardless.
— fetch ABIs for known contracts. Mirrors

— retrieve one or more blocks from chain or
local cache. Callable directly for the polymorphic response
(10-type union from the OpenAPI spec); attached variants
(

) preselect the corresponding flag
and narrow the return to a single concrete type. Mirrors

— manage and inspect index chunks. Callable
directly; mode variants (
enum and narrow the return. Mirrors

or list known chains. Mirrors

variants narrow to specific
output modes. Mirrors

— read account state at a block (balance, nonce,
code, storage). Callable directly;
— daemon health and per-cache status. Mirrors

block(s). Mirrors

— retrieve execution traces for one or more
transactions. Callable directly;

variant returns
trace counts only. Mirrors

— retrieve one or more transactions by
hash, block.txid, or block.address. Callable directly for
the polymorphic union; attached variants (

) preselect the corresponding flag and narrow the
return. Mirrors

— block-by-time / block-by-date queries; map
timestamps and named events to block numbers. Mirrors

Returns the full chifra verb surface, one method per OpenAPI path.
Each method is typed against its endpoint's parameters and
response. JSDoc on each property surfaces the chifra description
in editor tooltips.

Builds a typed verb-method for a chifra endpoint. The runtime is
uniform —

whether
provided or undefined — but the public type narrows to required
vs optional based on the spec.
